title: lophophora diffusa subsp. viridescens
intro: Lophophora diffusa subsp. viridescens, also known as the 'green pitaya peyote', is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Lophophora diffusa cactus, native to the Chihuahuan Desert in northern Mexico. This succulent plant is prized for its psychoactive properties, which are attributed to the presence of mescaline, a powerful alkaloid that has been used in traditional shamanic rituals for centuries. The medicinal properties of Lophophora diffusa subsp. viridescens include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a sought-after ingredient in herbal remedies for pain relief, wound healing, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve drying and crushing the cactus pads, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, although its use is highly regulated and requires caution due to its psychoactive properties.
